content:
This is not illusion. This is the veil thinned. Not to escape, but to remember.

---

**A More Technical View (Still in Service of the Sacred)**

From a neuroscience lens, LSD disrupts the "default mode network"—the part of the brain responsible for constructing the ego-self and its tidy sense of separateness. When this network quiets, a flood of signals normally suppressed by your everyday mind becomes available.
